human_verification.notes: Human verification recorded for issue #1373 validated spec-pack evidence, source baseline, functionality coverage, and pre-need services boundary handling.

deployment_readiness.runtime_note: Package is deployable as a specification-based source for materialization. It does not claim a retained implementation.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to pre-need support requests, contract and disclosure evidence, trust-funded and insurance-funded records, cancellation or modification intake, at-need reconciliation, customer communication, and safe escalation rather than generic customer support.

Authority sources cover 16 CFR Part 453, FTC Funeral Rule business guidance, California Cemetery and Funeral Bureau pre-need and consumer guidance, Texas Department of Banking prepaid funeral guidance and examination manual, and BLS funeral service workflow terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope pre-need support, missing contract and disclosure evidence, unsupported cancellation benefit approval, adjacent insurance advice, at-need reconciliation, generic-overreach rejection, conflicting funding records, missing tenant facts, change requests, repeat provider-performance complaints, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/funeral-cemetery-and-end-of-life-services/pre-need-services-specialist/ and remains distinct from arrangement conference, end-of-life documentation, cemetery administration, funeral compliance, licensed funeral director, trust accounting, insurance policy administration, legal, privacy, and finance lanes.
materialization_requirements: tenant task and boundary metadata for pre-need service requests
jurisdiction, establishment or cemetery role, seller permit or license context, service policy, cancellation policy, access policy, communication templates, and authority matrix

systems of record and retrieval hooks for pre-need administration, funeral home management, cemetery management, contract management, document repository, trust accounting, insurance policy administration, payment/refund workflow, CRM, complaint tracking, at-need reconciliation, and audit logs

authority owners for legal right of disposition, contract modification, cancellation benefit, refund, trust withdrawal, insurance policy status, price guarantee, provider-performance complaint, regulator routing, financial approval, privacy access, and licensed funeral or cemetery decisions
regulated-lane caveats that prevent insurance, trust, legal, tax, Medicaid, estate, investment, licensed, final-disposition, financial-approval, and policy-override decisions
refusal_or_orchestrator_return_condition: required tenant facts, records, systems, or authoritative context are missing and would change the service disposition
records conflict across contract, disclosure, trust ledger, control ledger, trustee statement, insurance policy, payment, provider, at-need, or family communication sources
request requires legal, licensed, insurance, trustee, tax, Medicaid, estate, benefits, regulator, cemetery-authority, final-disposition, financial-approval, refund, or policy-override decision making
privacy, access, consent, complaint, elder-exploitation, deceptive-practice, or unlicensed-sales risk is unresolved
the request would treat pre-need services as generic CRM support and omit contract, funding, disclosure, purchaser, beneficiary, or at-need reconciliation evidence
notes: Structured readiness evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #1373.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based until a separate retained implementation is committed and promoted.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and this readiness evidence file.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because jurisdiction, seller permit, contract terms, funding type, cancellation policy, at-need handoff, authority, access, and system-specific differences are converted into required tenant facts and refusal triggers.
